title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into the Ottauquechee River in Vermont.
The mother unbuckled her son from his car seat, cut through the airbag, and escaped the submerged vehicle through a window.
Both were evaluated at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center with no serious injuries reported; the crash cause is under investigation.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Amtrak Downeaster runs 143 miles from Boston to Brunswick, Maine, with 12 stations across Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and Maine.
The train offers reclining seats, free Wi-Fi, and an onboard cafe. Wheelchair accessibility and pet travel options are available.
One-way fares range from $3 to $38; seniors get 50% off. Reservations are required, and seats are unassigned.

title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Luke Murray, assistant coach at UConn, is set to lead Boston College basketball, replacing Earl Grant.
Dan Hurley praises Murray, predicting a renaissance for Boston College basketball under his leadership.
Luke Murray's hire could bring famous actor Bill Murray, his father, to Boston College basketball games.

title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA announces $80 round-trip fare for World Cup trains from South Station to Gillette Stadium, tickets sold via mTicket app.
Commuter Rail 'Boston Stadium Trains' will be the only direct transit to Gillette Stadium, operating with 14 express trains.
Summer fare discounts include free travel on Fridays and half-price monthly passes, with extended rail and bus services on match days.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix releases '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' a sequel featuring Ralph Fiennes and Alfie Williams.
'The Devil Wears Prada' and 'The Truman Show' are now streaming on HBO Max, Hulu, and Paramount+.
'The Drama,' a Boston-filmed dark comedy starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, receives mixed reviews.

title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Megan Whiting, 27, was shot by police in Northfield, N.H., after reportedly attacking a resident with a fire extinguisher.
Whiting was armed with a knife near an injured woman when an officer fired, resulting in her death. No officers were injured.
The officer's identity remains undisclosed, and body camera footage exists but won't be released until further investigation.

title: Signature Healthcare in Brockton hit by cybersecurity incident
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Signature Healthcare in Brockton experiencing a cybersecurity incident, affecting parts of their information systems.
Inpatient, emergency, and certain surgeries continue, but ambulances and chemotherapy infusions are canceled.
Retail pharmacies at Liberty St. and Donalds Way closed; patients asked to reschedule with Greene Cancer Center.

title: Villa Mexico Cafe is not closing after all
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Villa Mexico Cafe announced it will remain open after receiving a loan to pay overdue rent.
Julie King, founder, credited customer support and personal loans for securing their Financial District lease renewal.
The cafe is planning to extend hours and increase third-party delivery options to boost business.

title: Committee sues former GM over corporate theft of more than $1M: lawsuit
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Pier 50, LLC is suing former general manager Demetri Tsolakis for allegedly using over $1 million in corporate funds.
The lawsuit claims Tsolakis used Committee's funds for personal expenses and competing businesses, including misrepresenting ownership.
Tsolakis' attorney disputes allegations, asserting he lacked independent financial control and highlighting shared vendor practices.

title: Roman Anthony calls himself out for ‘bad baseball’ after latest poor throw
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony contributed to the Boston Red Sox's 8-6 loss with a poor throw leading to two runs.
Anthony, 21, admits to repeated poor throws affecting his performance despite strong batting skills.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora suggests tackling Anthony's throwing mechanics while considering him for more DH roles.
